small_time_deposits.notes: Monthly seasonally adjusted H.6 component series. Used in Stage 0 monetary diagnostics as the small-time-deposit subtraction for a more liquid-deposit target.

Best remaining public broad bank-deposit context candidate for the bank-only liquid-deposit decomposition, but still too broad to treat as a clean subcomponent because H.8 folds transaction deposits into this bucket.

reserve_balances_with_frb.description: Reserve Balances with Federal Reserve Banks: Week Average

fed_liquidity_credit_loans_net.description: Liquidity and Credit Facilities: Loans, Net: Wednesday Level

reverse_repo_treasury.description: Reverse Repurchase Agreements: Treasury Securities Sold by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
reverse_repo_treasury.notes: Daily New York Fed RRP series used as a liquidity-drain control in monetary Stage 1 diagnostics.

commercial_bank_borrowings.notes: Monthly H.8 series used in Stage 1 monetary diagnostics as a bank-funding context term.

commercial_bank_cash_assets.notes: Monthly H.8 series used in Stage 1 monetary diagnostics as a bank-liquidity context term.

foreign_official_custody_treasuries.description: Custody Holdings: Marketable U.S. Treasury Securities: Week Average
foreign_official_custody_treasuries.notes: Weekly H.4.1 memorandum series used in Stage 1 monetary diagnostics as a foreign-official custody context term. It is not treated as a clean signed control because of overlap risk with the ladder's own ROW Treasury channels.

foreign_related_treasury_agency_non_mbs.description: Treasury and Agency Securities: Non-MBS, Foreign-Related Institutions
foreign_related_treasury_agency_non_mbs.notes: Monthly H.8 foreign-related-institutions series used in Stage 1 monetary diagnostics as a foreign / portfolio-shift context term rather than a clean signed control.

gdp_deflator.description: Gross Domestic Product: Implicit Price Deflator

nominal_gdp_saar_bil.description: Gross Domestic Product, billions of dollars, seasonally adjusted annual rate

To align with estimator-scale quarterly millions, use `value_millions / (nominal_gdp_saar_bil * 250)`, which equals `(value_millions * 4) / (nominal_gdp_saar_bil * 1000)`.

tga_weekly.notes: TGA-only weekly diagnostic. Useful for high-frequency checks, but not a substitute for the Tier 0 Treasury operating cash term when historical Treasury Tax and Loan balances were material.

bea_row_fed_interest_paid_saar.description: Federal government current expenditures: Interest payments: to the rest of the world
bea_row_fed_interest_paid_saar.notes: Quarterly SAAR benchmark series from BEA via FRED. Useful for unit and scale checks against the rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxy.

bea_row_taxes_received_saar.description: Federal government current tax receipts: Taxes from the rest of the world
bea_row_taxes_received_saar.notes: Quarterly SAAR benchmark series from BEA via FRED. Use `billions * 250` to convert to estimator-scale quarterly millions.

bea_row_social_insurance_received_saar.description: Federal government current receipts: Contributions for government social insurance: From the rest of the world
bea_row_social_insurance_received_saar.notes: Quarterly SAAR benchmark series from BEA via FRED. Use `billions * 250` to convert to estimator-scale quarterly millions.

bea_row_current_transfer_receipts_receiv.description: Federal government current receipts: Current transfer receipts: From the rest of the world
bea_row_current_transfer_receipts_receiv.notes: Quarterly SAAR benchmark series from BEA via FRED. Use `billions * 250` to convert to estimator-scale quarterly millions.

fed_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.description: Quarterly Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy built from SOMA holdings snapshots.
fed_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. This is not downloaded from FRED; it is produced locally from SOMA holdings snapshots and used for Tier 1 Fed-corrected estimator variants.

bank_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.description: Quarterly bank-sector Treasury coupon-interest proxy for the default bank perimeter.
bank_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. This is not downloaded from FRED; it is produced locally and used for Tier 2 interest-corrected estimator variants. The default bank perimeter matches the Tier 0 bank-sector block: U.S.-chartered depositories, foreign banking offices in the U.S., and banks in U.S.-affiliated areas.

row_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. This is not downloaded from FRED; it is produced locally and used for Tier 2 interest-corrected estimator variants that include the rest-of-world Treasury term.

bank_noninterest_outlay_proxy.description: Quarterly Treasury noninterest outlay proxy to the default bank perimeter.
bank_noninterest_outlay_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. Positive values represent Treasury noninterest payments to the Tier 0 bank block and are subtracted in Tier 3 fiscal-corrected variants.

row_noninterest_outlay_proxy.description: Quarterly Treasury noninterest outlay proxy to the rest of world.
row_noninterest_outlay_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. Positive values represent Treasury noninterest payments to the rest of world and are subtracted in Tier 3 fiscal-corrected variants that include the ROW term.

bank_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.description: Quarterly Treasury nonborrow receipt proxy from the default bank perimeter.
bank_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. Positive values represent Treasury nonborrow receipts from the Tier 0 bank block and are added in Tier 3 fiscal-corrected variants.

row_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.description: Quarterly Treasury nonborrow receipt proxy from the rest of world.
row_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. Positive values represent Treasury nonborrow receipts from the rest of world and are added in Tier 3 fiscal-corrected variants that include the ROW term.

mint_cb_cash_factor_proxy.description: Quarterly mint or central-bank cash-factor proxy.
mint_cb_cash_factor_proxy.notes: Optional local support series. Positive values offset Treasury operating-cash changes that did not come from deposit-user taxes or deposit-user Treasury purchases and are added in Tier 3 fiscal-corrected variants.

credit_union_deposits.description: Quarterly federally insured credit-union shares and deposits bridge from NCUA Call Report ZIPs.
credit_union_deposits.notes: Optional local support series. This is a bridge-side support series for the bank-versus-broad-depository monetary diagnostic, built from NCUA quarterly Call Report `Acct_018` totals for federally insured credit unions.

thrift_deposits.description: Quarterly FDIC savings-institution deposit bridge from the banks financial API.
thrift_deposits.notes: Optional local support series. This is the thrift or savings-institution side of the bank-versus-broad-depository monetary diagnostic, built from FDIC `DEP` totals for BKCLASS `SB`, `SI`, and `SL`.

correction_series.tier1_fed_coupon_correction: Signed Tier 1 Fed coupon-interest correction. Negative values mean the corrected estimator is lower than Tier 0 by that amount in the quarter.
correction_series.tier2_bank_coupon_correction: Signed Tier 2 bank-sector coupon-interest correction layered on top of Tier 1 where available.
correction_series.tier2_row_coupon_correction: Signed Tier 2 rest-of-world coupon-interest correction layered on top of Tier 1 where available.
correction_series.tier3_bank_noninterest_outlay_correction: Signed Tier 3 bank noninterest outlay correction. Negative values mean Treasury noninterest payments to banks lower the corrected estimator relative to Tier 2.
correction_series.tier3_row_noninterest_outlay_correction: Signed Tier 3 ROW noninterest outlay correction.
correction_series.tier3_bank_nonborrow_receipt_correction: Signed Tier 3 bank nonborrow receipt correction. Positive values mean Treasury receipts from banks raise the corrected estimator relative to Tier 2.
correction_series.tier3_row_nonborrow_receipt_correction: Signed Tier 3 ROW nonborrow receipt correction.
correction_series.tier3_mint_cb_cash_factor_correction: Signed Tier 3 mint or central-bank cash-factor correction.

notes: The default headline is a bank-only transaction-based estimator beginning in late 2002.
A broad-depository alternative adds natural-person credit-union Treasury transactions and uses the same transaction-era coverage.
Corporate credit unions are included only as an additional sensitivity layer.
The aggregate credit-union sensitivity also includes the NCUA capitalization deposit, which is part of the published aggregate credit-union Treasury series in Z.1.
Negative H.4.1 remittance values are clipped to zero before quarter aggregation, and missing pre-history is treated as zero rather than suppressing older estimates.
The Tier 0 cash term is Treasury operating cash, not a TGA-only concept; historical Treasury Tax and Loan balances belong inside that operating-cash concept when they were material.
Tier 1 subtracts an optional Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy built from SOMA holdings snapshots.
Tier 2 keeps the same default bank-sector perimeter as Tier 0 and subtracts optional bank-sector and rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxies in addition to the Fed proxy.

Tier 3 keeps the Tier 2 interest layer and then applies optional reserve-user fiscal-flow corrections for bank and rest-of-world noninterest outlays, nonborrow receipts, and mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustments.
Separate historical-extension series splice in the level-change analog from 1990 until transaction coverage begins.
Level and decomposition methods are sensitivity checks only.

method_descriptions.tdc_base_bank_only_ru_flow: Preferred headline. Fed + bank-sector + rest-of-world Treasury transactions minus Treasury operating cash transactions plus positive Fed remittances. Transaction-data era only.
method_descriptions.tdc_base_broad_depository_np_cu_ru_flow: Broad-depository alternative that adds natural-person credit-union Treasury transactions. Transaction-data era only.
method_descriptions.tdc_broad_depository_np_corp_cu_ru_flow: Sensitivity that also adds corporate credit-union Treasury transactions.
method_descriptions.tdc_credit_union_aggregate_sensitivity: Sensitivity that matches the aggregate credit-union Treasury concept by adding natural-person and corporate credit-union Treasury transactions plus the NCUA capitalization deposit term.
method_descriptions.tdc_domestic_bank_only_ru_flow: Bank-only headline excluding the rest-of-world term.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_bank_only_ru_flo: Tier 1 bank-only variant. Tier 0 bank-only headline minus the optional Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy from SOMA holdings snapshots.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_broad_depository: Tier 1 broad-depository variant. Broad-depository Tier 0 headline minus the optional Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_domestic_bank_on: Tier 1 domestic-only bank variant. Domestic-only Tier 0 headline minus the optional Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_bank_only_r: Tier 2 bank-only variant. Tier 0 bank-only headline minus optional Fed, bank-sector, and rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxies.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_broad_depos: Tier 2 broad-depository variant. Broad-depository Tier 0 headline minus optional Fed, bank-sector, and rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxies.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_domestic_ba: Tier 2 domestic-only bank variant. Domestic-only Tier 0 headline minus optional Fed and bank-sector Treasury coupon-interest proxies.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_bank_only_ru_: Tier 3 bank-only variant. Tier 2 bank-only plus optional bank and rest-of-world reserve-user fiscal-flow corrections and a mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustment.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_broad_deposit: Tier 3 broad-depository variant. Tier 2 broad-depository plus optional bank and rest-of-world reserve-user fiscal-flow corrections and a mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustment.
method_descriptions.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_domestic_bank: Tier 3 domestic-only bank variant. Tier 2 domestic-only plus optional bank reserve-user fiscal-flow corrections and a mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustment.
method_descriptions.tdc_bank_only_extended_1990: Historical extension that uses the bank-only transaction headline where available and the bank-only level-change analog from 1990 until transaction coverage begins.
method_descriptions.tdc_broad_depository_extended_1990: Historical extension that uses the broad-depository transaction series where available and the broad-depository level-change analog from 1990 until transaction coverage begins.
method_descriptions.tdc_level_bank_only_sensitivity: Level-change version of the bank-only estimator.
method_descriptions.tdc_level_broad_depository_np_cu_sensiti: Level-change broad-depository sensitivity with natural-person credit unions.
method_descriptions.tdc_decomposition_proxy_bank_centric: Rough money/bank-balance-sheet proxy. Included for diagnostics only.

method_formulas.tdc_base_bank_only_ru_flow: Bank-only headline = Federal Reserve Treasury transactions + Bank-sector Treasury transactions + Rest-of-world Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash transactions + Positive Federal Reserve remittances.
method_formulas.tdc_base_broad_depository_np_cu_ru_flow: Broad-depository headline = Bank-only headline + Natural-person credit-union Treasury transactions.
method_formulas.tdc_broad_depository_np_corp_cu_ru_flow: Broad-depository plus corporate credit unions = Broad-depository headline + Corporate credit-union Treasury transactions.
method_formulas.tdc_credit_union_aggregate_sensitivity: Aggregate credit-union sensitivity = Broad-depository plus corporate credit unions + NCUA capitalization deposit term.
method_formulas.tdc_domestic_bank_only_ru_flow: Domestic-only bank headline = Federal Reserve Treasury transactions + Bank-sector Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash transactions + Positive Federal Reserve remittances.
method_formulas.tdc_no_remit_bank_only: Bank-only excluding remittances = Federal Reserve Treasury transactions + Bank-sector Treasury transactions + Rest-of-world Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash transactions.
method_formulas.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_bank_only_ru_flo: Tier 1 bank-only = Bank-only headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_broad_depository: Tier 1 broad-depository = Broad-depository headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier1_fed_corrected_domestic_bank_on: Tier 1 domestic-only = Domestic-only bank headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_bank_only_r: Tier 2 bank-only = Bank-only headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy - Bank-sector Treasury coupon-interest proxy - Rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_broad_depos: Tier 2 broad-depository = Broad-depository headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy - Bank-sector Treasury coupon-interest proxy - Rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier2_interest_corrected_domestic_ba: Tier 2 domestic-only = Domestic-only bank headline - Fed Treasury coupon-interest proxy - Bank-sector Treasury coupon-interest proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_bank_only_ru_: Tier 3 bank-only = Tier 2 bank-only - Bank noninterest outlay proxy - Rest-of-world noninterest outlay proxy + Bank nonborrow receipt proxy + Rest-of-world nonborrow receipt proxy + Mint or central-bank cash-factor proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_broad_deposit: Tier 3 broad-depository = Tier 2 broad-depository - Bank noninterest outlay proxy - Rest-of-world noninterest outlay proxy + Bank nonborrow receipt proxy + Rest-of-world nonborrow receipt proxy + Mint or central-bank cash-factor proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_tier3_fiscal_corrected_domestic_bank: Tier 3 domestic-only = Tier 2 domestic-only - Bank noninterest outlay proxy + Bank nonborrow receipt proxy + Mint or central-bank cash-factor proxy.
method_formulas.tdc_bank_only_extended_1990: Bank-only extended series = Bank-only headline when available; otherwise Bank-only level-change sensitivity.
method_formulas.tdc_broad_depository_extended_1990: Broad-depository extended series = Broad-depository headline when available; otherwise Broad-depository level-change sensitivity.
method_formulas.tdc_level_bank_only_sensitivity: Bank-only level-change sensitivity = Change in Federal Reserve Treasury holdings + Change in Bank-sector Treasury holdings + Change in Rest-of-world Treasury holdings - Change in Treasury operating cash + Positive Federal Reserve remittances.
method_formulas.tdc_level_broad_depository_np_cu_sensiti: Broad-depository level-change sensitivity = Bank-only level-change sensitivity + Change in natural-person credit-union Treasury holdings.
method_formulas.tdc_decomposition_proxy_bank_centric: Bank-centric decomposition proxy = Change in M2 - Change in currency - Change in non-Treasury bank assets.
method_formulas.tdc_base_bank_only_ru_flow_4q: Bank-only headline, four-quarter sum = Rolling four-quarter sum of the bank-only headline.
method_formulas.tdc_base_bank_only_ru_flow_cum: Bank-only headline, cumulative = Cumulative sum of the bank-only headline.

bank_perimeter.tier2_coupon_proxy_alignment: Tier 2 bank coupon corrections should align with the Tier 0 bank-sector block rather than a broader H.8-style perimeter.

cash_term.definition: Tier 0 subtracts Treasury operating cash, not a TGA-only cash balance.
cash_term.historical_treatment: When historical Treasury Tax and Loan balances were material, they belong inside the operating-cash concept rather than as a separate additive Tier 0 term.

fed_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.usage: Tier 1 Fed-corrected variants subtract this proxy from Tier 0.
fed_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.construction: Optional local support series produced from SOMA holdings snapshots and inferred coupon payment schedules. Raw SOMA par values are normalized to millions of U.S. dollars when needed.

bank_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.usage: Tier 2 corrected variants subtract this proxy from the same bank-sector block used in Tier 0.
bank_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.construction: Optional local support series produced outside the FRED downloader. The intended perimeter matches U.S.-chartered depositories, foreign banking offices in the U.S., and banks in U.S.-affiliated areas.

row_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.usage: Tier 2 bank-only and broad-depository variants subtract this proxy when the rest-of-world Treasury term is included.
row_tsy_coupon_interest_proxy.construction: Optional local support series produced outside the FRED downloader for the rest-of-world Treasury coupon-interest correction.

bank_noninterest_outlay_proxy.usage: Tier 3 variants subtract this proxy as Treasury noninterest payments to the default bank block.
bank_noninterest_outlay_proxy.construction: Optional local support series for Treasury noninterest outlays to banks. Positive values are treated as reserve-user fiscal payments and enter with a negative sign.

row_noninterest_outlay_proxy.usage: Tier 3 ROW-inclusive variants subtract this proxy as Treasury noninterest payments to the rest of world.
row_noninterest_outlay_proxy.construction: Optional local support series for Treasury noninterest outlays to the rest of world. Positive values enter with a negative sign.

bank_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.usage: Tier 3 variants add this proxy as Treasury nonborrow receipts from the default bank block.
bank_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.construction: Optional local support series for Treasury nonborrow receipts from banks. Positive values enter with a positive sign.

row_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.usage: Tier 3 ROW-inclusive variants add this proxy as Treasury nonborrow receipts from the rest of world.
row_nonborrow_receipt_proxy.construction: Optional local support series for Treasury nonborrow receipts from the rest of world. Positive values enter with a positive sign.

mint_cb_cash_factor_proxy.usage: Tier 3 variants add this proxy as a mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustment.
mint_cb_cash_factor_proxy.construction: Optional local support series for cash-balance changes that should offset Treasury operating-cash movements without being treated as deposit-user taxes or Treasury purchases.

value_units.nominal_example: 60,592 means 60,592 million dollars, or about $60.6 billion.
value_units.real_toggle: Optional latest-quarter-dollar restatement using the GDP implicit price deflator (GDPDEF).
value_units.percent_of_gdp_toggle: Optional percent-of-GDP view. Annualizes the quarterly TDC flow (in millions) and divides by nominal GDP (SAAR, billions): percent = value_millions * 4 / (nominal_gdp_saar_bil * 1000) * 100.

site.title: TDCest
site.tagline: Treasury-attributed component of deposits: estimator ladder, receipt boundaries, fiscal shell, and monetary cross-checks.
site.thesis: TDC should either add to deposits one-for-one or offset another deposit component one-for-one; this site compares the repo's transaction, fiscal-flow, and diagnostic proxy surfaces to show where that identity is strongest and where the remaining boundaries still sit.

rows.summary_note: Table 5.1 bank-minor bridge is now usable as a historical default view inside the age-eligible window.

Current bank bridge remains the leading candidate, but the latest share is too stale under the current rule (stale-share years = 4). No fresher official public IRS bank-minor share path beyond TY2022 has been identified. Stop gate: historical_default_only_current_nondefault.

Best current public recurring ROW pilot. The main bridge is now MRV-first, while secondary visa lines stay outside the main ROW delta until payer identity and cash timing are stronger. Latest secondary visa sensitivity: 16.845 million. Combined Statement now confirms the broader CBSP main-account family, but not the exact MRV sub-account. Promotion checklist: 3 complete, 1 partial default blocker, 2 missing required checks. Stop gate: stop_at_mrv_nondefault_pilot. Stronger nondefault FAH/FAM and OIG cash-route evidence is now loaded, but no public quarterly MRV cash series or global legal-remitter proof has been found.
Secondary recurring State-visa branch. Kept visible for scale and auditability, but it does not enter the main recurring ROW delta and is not in line for default promotion.

rows.plain_english_summary: The most direct theory statement: TDC is the Treasury-driven part of deposit change seen from the domestic nonbank deposit-user side.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: The repo does not directly observe every DU-side term. The live ladder approximates this identity from Treasury-security transactions, Treasury operating cash, remittances, and bounded fiscal corrections.
rows.main_caveat: This is the conceptual accounting identity. Current estimates are narrower measurement approximations, not a full direct DU ledger.

rows.latex: \Delta D^{TDC}_{DU} = (Q^T_{DU\to RU}-Q^T_{RU\to DU}) + (I^T + R^T_{RU} + \Pi^F_T - G^{ND}_{RU} - DS^T_{RU}) - \Delta TOC
rows.plain_english_summary: The main Treasury-cash identity: TDC depends on Treasury-security settlement with reserve-side counterparties, Treasury-related cash flows with those counterparties, and the change in Treasury operating cash.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the closest theory frame for the current live estimator ladder. The repo explicitly uses Treasury operating cash (TOC), Treasury-security transaction terms, and positive Fed remittances, then layers in narrower fiscal-flow corrections.
rows.main_caveat: The repo does not yet directly measure every RU-side issuance, receipt, outlay, and debt-service term in this exact symbolic form.

rows.display_title: Fed Remittances and Deferred Asset Treatment
rows.plain_english_summary: Negative Fed earnings do not imply Treasury pays the Fed. They build a deferred asset and suppress future remittances until that deferred asset is worked off.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: The repo already reflects this by clipping Fed remittances at zero before quarter aggregation and treating negative H.4.1 values as deferred-asset accounting rather than negative Treasury cash flow.
rows.main_caveat: The live measurement uses the public remittance series as a practical proxy rather than reconstructing the full Fed earnings identity term by term.

rows.latex: \Delta D^{TDC}_{DU} = (\Delta M-\Delta C-\Delta X) - (\Delta L^B_{DU}+\Delta A^{B,NT}_{DU}) - \Delta A^{CB,NT}_{DU} - \Delta F^{NT}_{DU} - \varepsilon
rows.plain_english_summary: A residual version of TDC: start from total deposit change, subtract major non-Treasury drivers, and treat the remainder as the Treasury contribution.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This maps to the repo’s monetary/disaggregated branch and decomposition proxies, which are used as diagnostics and cross-checks rather than as the headline estimator.
rows.main_caveat: The repo does not treat this residual system as a co-equal headline estimate because large perimeter and measurement wedges still remain.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{mkt,bank}_{TDC,t} = \Delta TS^{tx}_{Fed,t} + \Delta TS^{tx}_{Banks,t} + \Delta TS^{tx}_{ROW,t} - \Delta TOC^{tx}_{Treasury,t} + Remit^{+}_{Fed,t}
rows.plain_english_summary: The simplest live estimate: Treasury-security transactions plus positive Fed remittances minus Treasury operating cash.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the repo’s live bank-only base headline and the main implemented approximation to the Treasury-cash-constraint identity in the transaction-data era.
rows.main_caveat: It is still a measurement approximation. It is bank-only, marketable-Treasury-focused, and not a full direct implementation of the theory identities.

rows.plain_english_summary: The bank-only headline after removing coupon-interest distortions from Fed, banks, and ROW Treasury holdings.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the repo’s strongest transfer-side cleanup layer and one of the most informative live comparisons for downstream work.
rows.main_caveat: Coupon terms are still proxies built from public holdings and maturity inputs, not observed cash-settlement flows.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{Tier3,bank}_{TDC,t} = \widehat{\Delta D}^{Tier2,bank}_{TDC,t} - Outlay^{Banks}_t - Outlay^{ROW}_t + Receipt^{Banks}_t + Receipt^{ROW}_t + CashFactor_t
rows.plain_english_summary: Tier 2 plus narrow fiscal-flow corrections for bank and ROW outlays, bank and ROW receipts, and mint or central-bank cash factors.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the best live corrected estimate in the repo. It is the main fiscal-flow-corrected ladder output used for current-quarter work.
rows.main_caveat: Receipt-side cells remain partial. Current bank receipts stay nondefault, and MRV remains a bounded nondefault ROW pilot.

rows.plain_english_summary: A historical-only Tier 3 variant that adds the age-eligible IRS Table 5.1 bank receipt correction.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the repo’s strongest bank receipt result. It shows how Tier 3 changes when the bank receipt bridge is allowed inside the historical age-eligible window.
rows.main_caveat: The current-quarter bridge remains blocked by stale public IRS bank-minor shares, so this cannot be promoted into the live default estimate.

rows.plain_english_summary: A bounded rest-of-world receipt sensitivity using the MRV / CBSP recurring fee branch.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: This is the only serious recurring ROW receipt branch currently carried forward. It is kept separate from the live default estimate and used as a boundary marker.
rows.main_caveat: It remains blocked by legal-remitter or debited-account proof and by the lack of public quarterly MRV cash timing.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{decomp}_{TDC,t} \approx \Delta M_t - \Delta C_t - \Delta X_t - \text{non-Treasury deposit drivers}
rows.plain_english_summary: A diagnostic residual framework used to see whether the ladder is directionally plausible against broader deposit targets.
rows.current_measurement_mapping: The repo uses the depository target as the main monetary cross-check and the commercial-bank target as a stress test.
rows.main_caveat: This branch is intentionally not treated as the headline estimator because the unresolved wedge is still too large and too perimeter-dependent.
